subject: DevOps
title: The Three Ways
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Three Ways.

hook.text: DevOps rests on three principles called The Three Ways. They guide every practice in the movement.

buildup.text: The First Way: flow. Work moves left to right — from dev to ops to customer — without bottlenecks.

discovery.text: The Second Way: feedback. Information flows right to left so teams learn from production fast.

twist.text: The Third Way: continual learning. Experiment, fail safely, and improve through repetition.

climax.text: Every DevOps practice — CI/CD, monitoring, blameless postmortems — maps back to one of the Three Ways.

punchline.text: Flow, feedback, learning. Three ways, one philosophy.

quiz.question: What does the Second Way in DevOps focus on?
options: Feedback from production back to development
